query: Restructure the blending logic so that instead of a single composite, the user can adjust the contribution of each filter (saturation, invert, high-pass, sharpen, normal map) independently using float sliders, and have these weights control the final blended image.

desired.outcome: A single final output image that is a weighted sum of all filter outputs, with each weight controllable via a user-visible numeric input (float slider).
desired.quality: All original filter branches remain intact and unchanged; only the final merging stage is replaced with a multi-input weighted blend node; no dangling outputs; the original preview and save nodes still work with the new blended output.

_tags.author_rationale: This forces a structural redesign of the blending stage while keeping all filters intact, perfectly matching the big_adjustment type and high complexity.
